title: ResiliencePolicyContract
description: Language-agnostic contract for request retry/backoff parameters, retryable-error classification, and the SSE terminal-marker rule set, shared across forgecode (Rust), OmniRoute (TypeScript), and cliproxy (Go). Version: 1.0.0. This is the P5.3 expansion covering concerns 3 (retry/backoff) and 4 (SSE stop-signal). Reference implementations: forgecode `forge_config::RetryConfig` + `forge_app::retry::should_retry` (request retry), `forge_eventsource::retry::{ExponentialBackoff, DEFAULT_RETRY}` (SSE reconnect), and `forge_eventsource::is_sse_terminal` (stop rule). The backoff loop itself stays language-native (Rust `backon`, TS/Go bespoke); only the parameter set and the rule set are contractual.

RetryPolicy.description: Exponential-backoff-with-jitter parameters for retrying outbound requests. Reference: forgecode `forge_config::RetryConfig`.

max_attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts after the initial try. Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.max_attempts` (usize); OmniRoute `MAX_REQUEST_RETRY` (=10).

initial_backoff_ms.description: Initial backoff delay in milliseconds before the first retry. Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.initial_backoff_ms`.

min_delay_ms.description: Minimum delay in milliseconds between attempts (floor applied to the backoff curve). Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.min_delay_ms`, fed to backon `with_min_delay`.

max_delay_secs.description: Upper cap on the per-attempt delay in seconds. Null = no cap. Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.max_delay_secs`.

backoff_factor.description: Multiplier applied to the delay on each successive attempt (delay_n = delay_{n-1} * backoff_factor). Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.backoff_factor` fed to backon `with_factor`; OmniRoute `minRetryCooldownMs * 2^(failures-1)` (factor 2).

jitter.description: Whether to apply randomized jitter to each computed delay to avoid thundering-herd retries. Reference: forgecode `ExponentialBuilder::with_jitter()`; cliproxy `backoffWithJitter`.

suppress_errors.description: Whether to suppress retry error logging/telemetry events for expected retries. Reference: forgecode `RetryConfig.suppress_errors`.

RetryableClassification.description: Rules that decide whether a failed request is eligible for retry. An error is retryable when EITHER its HTTP status is in retryable_status_codes OR its classified error kind is in retryable_error_kinds. Reference: forgecode `forge_app::retry::should_retry` gates on the domain `Error::Retryable` variant, and `RetryConfig.status_codes` carries the HTTP set.

retryable_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes that trigger a retry. Default matches forgecode `RetryConfig.status_codes` ([429, 500, 502, 503, 504, 408, 522, 524, 520, 529]) — 408 timeout, 429 rate-limit, 5xx server errors, and Cloudflare-class 520/522/524/529.

retryable_error_kinds.description: Transport/error kinds that trigger a retry independent of HTTP status (e.g. before a status is ever received). These correspond to errors a runtime maps onto forgecode's `Error::Retryable`.

non_retryable_status_codes.description: Client-error statuses that MUST NOT be retried (retrying cannot succeed without a request change). Advisory: a non-retryable status always wins over retryable classification.

SseReconnectPolicy.description: Backoff parameters for reconnecting a dropped SSE stream (distinct from request retry). Reference: forgecode `forge_eventsource::retry::DEFAULT_RETRY` = ExponentialBackoff { start: 300ms, factor: 2.0, max_duration: 5s, max_retries: None }.

start_ms.description: Initial reconnect delay in milliseconds. Reference: DEFAULT_RETRY start = 300 ms. A server-sent `retry:` field overrides this at runtime.

factor.description: Exponential multiplier per reconnect attempt. Reference: DEFAULT_RETRY factor = 2.0.

max_delay_ms.description: Cap on the reconnect delay in milliseconds. Reference: DEFAULT_RETRY max_duration = 5 s.

max_retries.description: Maximum reconnect attempts; null = unbounded. Reference: DEFAULT_RETRY max_retries = None.

honor_server_retry_field.description: Whether a server-sent SSE `retry:` value resets the reconnect delay. Reference: forge_eventsource sets reconnection time from the event's retry field.

SseStopRule.description: The terminal-marker rule set that decides when an SSE stream has ended. Reference: forgecode `forge_eventsource::is_sse_terminal(data)` = `matches!(data, "[DONE]" | "")`, plus the OpenAI/Anthropic finish-field rules aligned across repos (OmniRoute `checkIfStopSignal`, cliproxy `stream_forwarder`).

terminal_data_values.description: SSE event `data:` field values that signal end-of-stream. '[DONE]' is the canonical OpenAI/Anthropic sentinel; '' (empty) is a keepalive / implicit-end marker. Reference: forgecode `is_sse_terminal`.

openai_finish_reasons.description: Values of `choices[0].finish_reason` that indicate the model has finished generating in an OpenAI-style stream.

anthropic_stop_fields.description: Anthropic SSE event fields carrying the stop reason; a non-null value signals completion.

synthetic_done_on_silent_close.description: When the upstream connection closes without an explicit terminal event, implementations MUST emit a synthetic '[DONE]' terminal signal rather than propagating an unexpected EOF to callers. Reference: cliproxy openai_compat_executor injects synthetic [DONE] on silent close.

description: Top-level resilience contract document. retry_policy + retryable_classification govern outbound request retries; sse_reconnect_policy governs stream reconnection; sse_stop_rule governs stream termination.
